text: There is low overall evidence to suggest a relationship between foot strike technique and running-related injuries.
Three of six categorical studies found rearfoot strikers have a significantly greater retrospective injury rate than non-rearfoot strikers.

When foot strike technique is measured as a continuous variable (foot contact angle, ankle flexion angle, strike index), only 1 of 7 studies found a significant relationship with running-related injuries.

Studies finding a relationship between foot strike pattern and injury were very low quality, with limitations including retrospective design, low participant numbers, and poor foot strike assessment methods.
Foot strike technique alone should not be used as a primary target for injury prevention interventions, as current evidence is insufficient to support a causal relationship with injuries.
